title: VBA OnAction scope mismatch for synthetic workbook controls
originSignal: A workbook control has an OnAction target whose module scope, workbook qualification, or public entry point is ambiguous.
riskPattern: A control can reference a procedure name that exists in source text but is not callable from the workbook control at runtime.
regressionChecks: Inventory synthetic control OnAction strings and their resolved procedure targets.
Lint public entry points and identify scope or qualification mismatches.
Keep static binding findings separate from Windows Excel macro execution evidence.
expectedEvidence: Button binding report flags unresolved or scope-mismatched synthetic OnAction targets.
VBA lint report records public procedure and source-level findings.
Inventory evidence identifies the control and target string without private workbook content.
boundaries: Static inspection cannot prove Trust Center access, macro enablement, or button-click behavior.
The case uses deterministic synthetic control names and contains no customer VBA artifacts.
nextLiveWorkbookRequirement: Open a sanitized xlsm in Windows Excel, enable macros under the intended policy, and run a harmless OnAction smoke test.
